revision.text:
[[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}・{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|250px]] | location = Shimosakura 304, Misato-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kawa-no-Kami|ja|川神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawa-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}} (River deity) at Kawara Shrine
{{ill|Mori-no-Kami|ja|木神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}} (Tree deity) at Mori Shrine | type = Kawara Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}}

[[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}・{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|250px]] | location = Shimosakura 304, Misato-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kawa-no-Kami|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawa-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}} (River deity) at Kawara Shrine
{{ill|Mori-no-Kami|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}} (Tree deity) at Mori Shrine | type = Kawara Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}}

Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}・{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|250px]] | location = Shimosakura 304, Misato-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kawa-no-Kami|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawa-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}} (River deity) at Kawara Shrine
{{ill|Mori-no-Kami|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}} (Tree deity) at Mori Shrine | type = Kawara Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}・{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|250px]] | location = Shimosakura 304, Misato-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kawa-no-Kami|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawa-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}} (River deity) at Kawara Shrine
{{ill|Mori-no-Kami|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}} (Tree deity) at Mori Shrine | type = Kawara Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228197]] Wikidata name: Kawara Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}・{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|250px]] | location = Shimosakura 304, Misato-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kawa-no-Kami|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawa-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}} (River deity) at Kawara Shrine
{{ill|Mori-no-Kami|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}} (Tree deity) at Mori Shrine | type = Kawara Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*)
Mori Shrine: {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} [[File:Kawara-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Sacred grove of Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine]] '''{{ill|Kawara Shrine (Geku)|ja|河原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine (Geku)|qq=}}''' (*Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the '''{{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}''' (*Mori Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kawara Shrine. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Misato-cho Shinkai, enshrined in a forest surrounded by {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}} to the north of {{ill|Garyūbai Park|ja|臥竜梅公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garyūbai Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its connection to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} and the Garyūbai plum treeIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 45. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. Like {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, which is also an auxiliary shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}, and {{ill|Mori Shrine|ja|毛理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河原神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228197}}, which is enshrined together with Kawara Shrine, the three are collectively known as the "Mitsu-sha" (Three Shrines). According to ancient texts, Kawara Shrine is located north of Kawarabuchi ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 911. Some theories suggest that Kawara Shrine was originally situated at the site of Hinokijiri Shrine along the Hinokijiri River in {{ill|Funae, Ise|ja|船江 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funae, Ise|1=Funae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213313}}, but during the Edo period, when Kawarabuchi Shrine was restored, Hinokijiri Shrine was identified with Kawarabuchi Shrine's site. As a result, Kawara Shrine was reconstructed further north in Misato-cho Shinkai, Ise. == Kawara Shrine == Kawara Shrine Ranks 11th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is the {{ill|River deity|ja|川神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579704}}. The deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the lower reaches of the [[:en:Miya River (Mie)|Miyagawa River]] and also protects the rice fieldsGakken Publishing (2013): p. 63. Although the enshrined deity of the former site of Hinokijiri Shrine in Funae, Ise, was unknown, it was decided to enshrine the River deity from Kawara Shrine due to its historical connectionUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 914. Hinokijiri Shrine was relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Mori Shrine == Mori Shrine Ranks 6th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine. Its enshrined deity is the {{ill|Tree deity|ja|木神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tree deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579705}}. This deity is regarded as the spirit of the [[:en:Shinboku|sacred tree]] in the forest where the shrine is located. It is believed that the site of Mori Shrine corresponds to the former location of Shigai Shrine in Misato-cho ShinkaiUjiyamada City Office (1929): vol. 2, p. 912. Shigai Shrine was later relocated to the same site as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == History == According to the definition of Ise Jingū's subsidiary shrines, Mori Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishikichō|lt=Enryaku Gishiki-chō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishiki-chō|qq=}}, i.e., before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines, Kawara Shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. During the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals were discontinued, and by the [[:en:Early modern period|early modern period]], the shrine sites had become unknown. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) restored the shrines at their current locationsFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20150610132106/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080144-01.html Kawara Shrine]" (Accessed September 29, 2013). == Flora == A large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] stands behind the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}. The {{ill|sacred precinct|ja|神域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred precinct|1=shin'iki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588853}} is well-maintained with neatly trimmed hedges. == Access and Surroundings == Next to Kawara Shrine and Mori Shrine is a [[:en:Factory|factory]] operated by Matsuya Confectionery, known for producing black candyIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 49. A 10-minute walk north along Ise City Road will lead to the site of the former {{ill|Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja|山田奉行所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Magistrate's Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田奉行|1=Yamada bugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4275043}}. * About a 6-minute walk (approx. 500 m) from the "Shinkai" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]] serviced by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. * Approximately a 2-minute drive (approx. 800 m) north from the "Ogicho 1" [[:en:Intersection (road)|intersection]] on [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] ({{ill|Nansei Bypass|ja|南勢バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nansei Bypass|1=Nansei Bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407535}}). No parking is available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - [[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（内宮）摂社。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0403.html 河原神社（かわらじんじゃ） 御同座 毛理神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (伊勢市)|version=100175820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:河原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228197}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
